operational_knowledge_units.title: Idle AGV can be manually sent to a selected square using Go-to
operational_knowledge_units.summary: The slide states that an AGV not currently in a task can be sent to any square using the Go-to function.
observed_evidence: Slide text: 'An AGV not in a task can be sent to any square.'
Numbered UI sequence references selecting an AGV, Go to, square, and Confirm
operational_knowledge_units.retrieval_text: Robot Selection Go-to function lets an AGV that is not in a task be sent to any square. The UI flow shown is Select an AGV, Select Go to, Select which square you want the AGV to go to, then Select Confirm.

operational_knowledge_units.title: Go-to function is presented as a quick recovery aid for stranded AGVs
operational_knowledge_units.summary: The slide describes the Go-to function as helpful for quick recovery, with an example where a tipper goes down and an AGV will not leave. The operator can remove and add the AGV and use Go-to to drop it off at the hospital.
observed_evidence: Slide text: 'This is helpful when trying to recover quickly.'
Slide text: 'Example if a Tipper goes down and an AGV will not leave.'
Slide text: 'You can remove and add the agv and use the go to function to drop it off at the hospital.'

relationships.target: Tipper goes down and AGV will not leave

operational_knowledge_units.retrieval_text: The Go-to function is described as useful for quick AGV recovery. Example given: if a Tipper goes down and an AGV will not leave, the AGV can be removed and added, then sent with Go-to to the hospital.

operational_knowledge_units.title: AGV picks up a new task after its tote has been removed
operational_knowledge_units.summary: The slide states that after the AGV is dropped off, it will pick up a new task once its tote has been removed.
observed_evidence: Slide text: 'The AGV will pickup a new task once it’s tote has been removed'
operational_knowledge_units.retrieval_text: According to the slide, an AGV will pick up a new task once its tote has been removed.

operational_knowledge_units.title: Go-to UI requires AGV selection, destination square selection, and confirmation
operational_knowledge_units.summary: The slide's numbered instructions indicate the main UI elements involved in sending an AGV to a square.
observed_evidence: Slide text: '3. Select which square you want the AGV to go to'
operational_knowledge_units.retrieval_text: UI concept for Go-to: select an AGV, choose Go to, choose the square destination, and confirm.

procedure_candidate_attempts.title: Send an AGV to a selected square using Go-to
preconditions: The AGV is not in a task
procedure_candidate_attempts.procedure_visual_summary: Slide shows Robot Selection / Go-to function with numbered action cues: 1 select an AGV, 2 select Go to, 3 select destination square, 4 select Confirm.

steps.expected_outcome: An AGV is selected in the Robot Selection interface.
steps.validation_check: The intended AGV is highlighted or otherwise shown as selected.
steps.visual_region_hint: UI callout labeled 1 near AGV selection area

steps.expected_outcome: The Go-to action is opened for the selected AGV.
steps.validation_check: The Go-to option is activated for the selected AGV.
steps.visual_region_hint: UI callout labeled 2 near Go to control

steps.instruction: Select which square you want the AGV to go to.
steps.expected_outcome: A destination square is chosen for the AGV.
steps.validation_check: The intended square is selected as the destination.
steps.visual_region_hint: UI callout labeled 3 near square selection area

steps.expected_outcome: The Go-to command is submitted and the AGV is sent to the selected square.
steps.validation_check: The destination is confirmed and the AGV is assigned to go to the chosen square.
steps.visual_region_hint: UI callout labeled 4 near Confirm control

warnings: This procedure applies only to an AGV not in a task.
procedure_candidate_attempts.retrieval_text: Procedure to send an idle AGV to a square using Go-to: select an AGV, select Go to, choose the destination square, and select Confirm. Slide states this applies when the AGV is not in a task.
